page: Amazon Web Services r7a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 processors built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and includes a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps. No GPUs are present on this instance.

In benchmark evaluations, the r7a.12xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, placing in the top 10% for multi-core stress-ng tests, Geekbench multi-core scores, and software compilation tasks. Memory bandwidth is strong, with uncached read speeds reaching 223.43 GB/sec and write speeds at 136.22 GB/sec. The processor's instruction set, which includes AVX-512 and cryptography extensions like AES and SHA_NI, supports strong performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and DDR5 memory architecture, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The absence of bundled local storage means storage requirements must be met via network-attached options, which is a key architectural tradeoff. The instance is highly suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, software compilation pipelines, and medium-scale language model inference tasks.
description: The Amazon Web Services r7a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores operating at 3.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, delivering 8.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps. In benchmark testing, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for software compilation and image processing, alongside strong memory bandwidth and database operation speeds. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads relying on network storage. It is highly suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and software development pipelines.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 48 AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 19 Gbps network baseline, optimized for in-memory databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 48 AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86 platform featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM and dedicated AMD EPYC processors for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 cores running at 3.7 GHz
384 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU and software compilation performance
19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and memory-heavy workloads
